children.value: US employment authorization required for program duration
application → technical assessment → take-home → mentor discussion on research and project directions
PhD in a STEM field, or equivalent research experience
Familiarity with Claude and Claude Code “in regular workflow” (named explicitly as a requirement)
Capacity to deliver concrete results within the fellowship timeframe
Enthusiasm about AI accelerating scientific discovery
Note: “ML background is advantageous but not required; scientific judgment and learning agility are prioritized.” Anthropic explicitly invites applications from candidates who don’t meet every line.
Everything is inline in the Greenhouse form except the CV.
No word or character limits are stated
on any of the questions; the fields are textareas. No separate research-statement upload.
First name, Last name, Email, Phone (opt), Country.
Resume/CV — PDF, DOC, DOCX, TXT, RTF. Direct upload, Dropbox, or Google Drive.
“Please share the STEM field(s) where you have the most expertise and describe your relevant experience/contribution.”
“Describe any experience you have with ML/AI, e.g. training custom models, applying an existing model, using adjacent techniques, etc.”
“Describe any experience you have with Claude Code or similar agentic coding products. If you have used them before, please describe your use case(s) and workflow.”

“Propose one or two concrete ideas for evaluating, improving, or applying Claude in your field that you think are promising and would be excited about. (This is not necessarily what you will work on – the mentors will help co-design the actual project – but it helps us understand your interests.)”
“Provide two references that can speak to your past work and experience. Please include their name, email address (or preferred contact method), and any additional context about your collaboration.”
The CNRS application is a ~25-page dossier pitched to a French AI/CS committee, with a 5pp research programme as the centerpiece and a counter-theme of thoroughness over speed.
This one is the opposite shape:
No long-form research statement, no programme, no 5pp.
Audience knows AI deeply, not cosmology.
The framing flips: CNRS sold cosmology-with-AI to AI people; this sells
the cosmologist who is already deep in real Claude-Code-mediated frontier science
Three months on-site. Not a research programme — a fellowship engagement collaborating with research teams.
Mentors co-design. So the project-ideas answer is a signal of

The CNRS dossier is a strong source for: bibliometrics, the UNIONS B-mode story, the agentic-science framing, the verification/tapestry threads, the SPT × Euclid MoU language, the publications narrative. Lift what fits; reshape voice for the new audience.
Three principles, paraphrased then quoted where exact:
AI as collaborator to strengthen applications.
Refine, don’t replace. Authenticity over polish.
Same honesty Anthropic applies to its own processes.
Allowed: polishing your own drafts, refining articulation, researching Anthropic, practicing interviews.
Not allowed: AI on coding assessments (unless explicitly permitted), fabricating experience, live-interview assistance, AI substituting for original thinking.

Implication for this application: Claude can polish what Cail has actually done. It cannot manufacture project ideas Cail hasn’t reasoned about, or invent Claude Code workflows Cail hasn’t lived. The Claude Code Usage question in particular wants lived practice — and Cail has unusually deep, well-documented lived practice (felt, shuttle, lightcone, the UNIONS workflow, this very conversation).

frontmatter.name: Anthropic STEM Fellowship — call requirements
frontmatter.outcome: Single source of truth for the application — 5 short-essay questions + CV + 2 references, no word limits, due May 15. Tight, focused, very different shape from CNRS.
